urgency.message: 2 critical CVE(s) and 7 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Chromium: CVE-2026-11631 Use after free in Aura
items.summary: This CVE was assigned by Chrome.  Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) ingests Chromium, which addresses this vulnerability. Please see [Google Chrome Releases](https://chromereleases.googleblog.com/2025) for more information.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 16th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9974, (Tue, Jun 16th)
UK to ban social media access for children under 16
items.summary: The ban will apply to all “user-to-user platforms, whose purpose is to enable social interaction and which allow users to post material, alongside algorithms,” according to a press release from the government’s Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.

items.title: Inside the Modern SOC: The 72-Minute Race
items.summary:
Attackers can move from access to exfiltration in 72 minutes. Learn how modern SOC teams close the speed gap with Unit 42's AI-driven automation, threat hunting, MDR and Managed XSIAM.
The post Inside the Modern SOC: The 72-Minute Race appeared first on Unit 42.

items.title: DOJ seizes CFAKE, SOCFAKE deepfake nude sites under TAKE IT DOWN Act
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice announced Friday that it has seized the CFAKE.com and SOCFAKE.com websites, which allegedly hosted nonconsensual AI-generated nude images and videos of women, in what appears to be the first publicly announced domain seizure under the TAKE IT DOWN Act. [...]

items.title: SimpleHelp bug lets hackers create rogue remote support accounts
items.summary: A vulnerability in the SimpleHelp remote management software allows unauthenticated attackers to create privileged technician accounts on servers using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication protocol. [...]

items.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
items.summary:
A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email.

The way in was a backdoor on their REDCap research servers that stole login credentials. The exfiltration was the u

items.title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two malicious cyber campaigns that exhibit similarities with a persistent North Korean threat cluster known as Contagious Interview (aka Famous Chollima, HexagonalRodent, and Void Dokkaebi).

According to a report published by Proofpoint, the threat actor has b

items.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
items.summary: The critical, three-stage attack is now patched, but it's part of a new group of AI prompt-injection issues that use hidden URLs and other variables.

items.title: Maine closes data breach portal to the public after fake reports
items.summary: Maine is still allowing companies to report breaches, but won’t make the portal easily available to the public until after it completes an audit of its procedures to stop such incidents, according to a press release from the Maine attorney general’s office.

items.title: OptinMonster WordPress plugin hacked in CDN supply-chain attack
items.summary: WordPress plugins OptinMonster, TrustPulse, and PushEngage have been compromised in a supply-chain attack impacting Awesome Motive-s content distribution network (CDN). [...]

items.title: Cisco fixes SD-WAN vManage flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
items.summary: Cisco has released security updates to address a vulnerability in the Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, that was exploited in attacks to escalate to root privileges. [...]

items.title: China-Nexus Actor Spy on US Researchers Undetected for a Year
items.summary: Google discovered and disrupted the sprawling campaign, which stole RedCAP credentials to target numerous institutions and exfiltrate sensitive data.

items.title: Attackers Hijack Popular WordPress Plugins to Deploy Backdoors
items.summary: Tampered OptinMonster and sister plugins plant hidden backdoors on 1.2 million WordPress sites

items.title: Most CISOs Report Pressure to Bury Bad Security News
items.summary: Executive leaders may not be saying it aloud, but business objectives and priorities don't always promote timely disclosures.

items.title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
items.summary:
A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed

LiteLLM is a widely deployed open-source AI gateway that brokers calls to more than 100 model providers behind one Open

items.title: Council of Europe investigates ShinyHunters data breach claims
items.summary: The Council of Europe, the continent's oldest intergovernmental body, is probing claims of a data breach made by the ShinyHunters extortion group over the weekend. [...]

items.title: Adriatic Port Cyber-Attack by Anubis Sparks Warning Over Maritime Security Risks
items.summary: How the Anubis ransomware group stole and leaked an Italian Adriatic port authority's data

items.title: What Changed in OWASP Top 10 2025 and Recommendations for Each Category
items.summary: Key Takeaways  The OWASP Foundation has released the eighth edition of its Top 10 Web Application Security Risks. This is the first major update since 2021, and was built on analysis of more than 175,000 CVE records and 589 Common Weakness Enumerations. Two entirely new OWASP Top 10 2025 catego

items.title: FBI: Fraudsters use couriers to steal money in crypto scams
items.summary: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned that criminals are using couriers to collect money from victims of cryptocurrency investment scams, also known as pig butchering or romance baiting. [...]

items.title: Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer
items.summary:
Mackay Sugar was targeted in a cyberattack carried out by a threat group known as The Gentlemen.
The post Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
items.summary:
A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search.

Researchers at Varonis Threat Labs chained three bugs into a one-click exfiltration path they call SearchLeak. Because the link poin

items.title: The Beginning of the End of Social Engineering
items.summary: AI-native operating systems are shifting the responsibility to stay vigilant against social engineering cyberattacks from the user onto the system itself.

items.title: Cyberattack on Russian tech firm Astral disrupts business, government services for week
items.summary: According to customer complaints, the disruption affected a range of services used by businesses, leading to interruptions in cash register operations, difficulties selling certain regulated goods, loss of access to customer portals and corporate email and problems with electronic human resources do

items.title: Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America
items.summary:
Google’s Threat Intelligence Group has been tracking the cyberespionage group as UNC6508 since early 2025.
The post Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Vibe coders are gonna vibe code: How CISOs are tackling code sprawl
items.summary: Employees are increasingly building automations, agents, and apps with AI tools outside traditional security oversight. Tines explores how CISOs are handling AI-driven code sprawl, shadow tooling, and governance challenges. [...]

items.title: Public and Private Medical Community Targeted by China-Nexus Threat Actor Pursuing Artificial Intelligence, Cyber, Medical, and National Defense Research
items.summary:
Written by: Patrick Whitsell, John McGuiness

Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has identified a sophisticated campaign attributed to UNC6508, a People's Republic of China (PRC)-nexus threat actor, targeting institutions in the North American academic, medical, and military research community.

items.title: Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers, steal medical research
items.summary: A China-linked espionage campaign targeted exposed REDCap servers to deploy the InfiniteRed malware and steal sensitive data from a medical institution in North America. [...]

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Day, UniFi Exploits, macOS Stealers, VPN Flaw and More
items.summary:
Stuff broke again. Not in a movie way. An old tool was left exposed. An abandoned package was abused. A deprecated feature was still running in prod.

This week is the same lesson in a new form: phishing kits are easier to rent, AI names are useful bait, old login paths still fail, and forgotten sof

items.title: 15th June – Threat Intelligence Report
items.summary: For the latest discoveries in cyber research for the week of 15th June, please download our Threat Intelligence Bulletin. TOP ATTACKS AND BREACHES The University of Nottingham, a UK research university, has suffered a data breach after ShinyHunters accessed its student records system. The incident a

items.title: Finland brings charges against cargo ship officers for cutting submarine cables
items.summary: According to the deputy prosecutor general, the ship’s officers have now been charged with “having damaged two subsea telecommunications cables and of having attempted to damage a total of eight other subsea connections.”

items.title: NewCore Emerges From Stealth Mode With $66 Million in Funding
items.summary:
The startup has built a security-first identity platform to protect humans, machines, and AI agents.
The post NewCore Emerges From Stealth Mode With $66 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic says US government forced it to disable cybersecurity AI models
items.summary: According to the company, the directive cited national security authorities. It appears to be the first time such authorities have been used to curtail the export of AI models rather than chips or hardware.

items.title: US Cracks Down on Anthropic AI Models Amid Abuse Concerns
items.summary: Anthropic abruptly suspended all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after receiving an export control directive that banned foreign nationals from using the technology.

items.title: Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges
items.summary:
Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko admitted to working on the development of a loader for the Conti gang.
The post Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: The Onboarding Password Mistake That Creates Unnecessary Risk
items.summary:
Employee onboarding is a busy time for IT teams. New starters need devices, accounts, access permissions, and passwords, all delivered within a tight timeframe.

That usually means sharing a temporary "first-day" password so employees can access systems for the first time. The issue is that these pa

items.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems
items.summary:
The pharmaceutical giant says the attackers gained access to personal data stored on the compromised systems. 
The post Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker
items.summary:
French officials say roughly 73,000 government accounts were affected, while the threat actor claims to have stolen messages and user data from the sovereign Tchap platform.
The post French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: 152 Chrome Wallpaper Extensions with 105K Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Traffic
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a network of 152 Google Chrome extensions that act as new tab live wallpaper add-ons to distribute a potentially unwanted program (PUP) family.

The cluster spans 38 separate Chrome Web Store publisher accounts and three brand backends: tabplugins[.]com, yow

items.title: ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack
items.summary:
The extortion group threatens to leak 297 GB of data allegedly stolen from the Council of Europe, including employee personal information.
The post ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cybersecurity Experts Urge US to Lift Ban on Anthropic's Frontier AI Models
items.summary: Access to two Anthropic large language models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, has effectively been banned to any non-US nationals by the Trump administration

items.title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
items.summary:
An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript files used by WordPress sites running PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning those files into a way to break into the sites.

When a site administrator was logged in as the file loaded, the code created an admin account under the attacker's con

items.title: FBI, Google Dismantle ‘Outsider Enterprise’ Phishing Service
items.summary:
The platform used more than 9,000 phishing sites, stealing nearly 4 million credit cards and causing roughly $1.9 billion in losses.
The post FBI, Google Dismantle ‘Outsider Enterprise’ Phishing Service appeared first on SecurityWeek.
